title: Student cannot access another student's course data
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Student account 'alice' owns course with id 'course-alice-1'
Student account 'bob' has a valid session token
steps: Send GET /api/student/courses/course-alice-1 with bob's session token in the Authorization header
expected_result: HTTP 403 response; no course data belonging to alice is returned.

title: Student can access their own course data
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Student account 'alice' owns course with id 'course-alice-1'
alice has a valid session token
steps: Send GET /api/student/courses/course-alice-1 with alice's session token in the Authorization header
expected_result: HTTP 200 response; the response body contains alice's course data.

title: Student cannot access another student's submission
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Student account 'alice' has a submission with id 'sub-alice-1'
Student account 'bob' has a valid session token
steps: Send GET /api/student/submissions/sub-alice-1 with bob's session token in the Authorization header
expected_result: HTTP 403 response; no submission data belonging to alice is returned.

title: Student cannot access another student's grades
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Student account 'alice' has a grade record with id 'grade-alice-1'
Student account 'bob' has a valid session token
steps: Send GET /api/student/grades/grade-alice-1 with bob's session token in the Authorization header
expected_result: HTTP 403 response; no grade data belonging to alice is returned.

title: Student cannot access another student's chat history
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Student account 'alice' has chat history entries for course 'course-alice-1'
Student account 'bob' has a valid session token
steps: Send GET /api/student/courses/course-alice-1/chat with bob's session token in the Authorization header
expected_result: HTTP 403 response; no chat history belonging to alice is returned.

title: Data isolation enforced at the database query level
preconditions: Two student records exist in the test database with distinct student_id values
steps: Call the course-query function with student_id set to alice's id
Assert that all returned records have owner_student_id equal to alice's id
expected_result: Every record in the result set has owner_student_id matching alice's id; no records belonging to another student are present.

title: Three password-reset requests within one hour all succeed
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
User account 'alice' exists
No password-reset requests have been made for 'alice' in the last hour
steps: POST /api/auth/password-reset with body {"username":"alice"} three times in sequence
expected_result: All three requests return HTTP 200 or HTTP 202; each triggers a reset credential delivery.

title: Fourth password-reset request within one hour is rejected
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
User account 'alice' exists
Three password-reset requests have already been made for 'alice' within the current hour
steps: POST /api/auth/password-reset with body {"username":"alice"}
expected_result: HTTP 429 response; the request is rejected and no additional reset credential is issued.

title: Password-reset rate limit resets after one hour elapses
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Rate-limit window is configured to 5 seconds for the test
User account 'alice' exists and has already exhausted the three-request limit
steps: Wait for the rate-limit window to elapse
POST /api/auth/password-reset with body {"username":"alice"}
expected_result: HTTP 200 or HTTP 202 response; the request succeeds after the rate-limit window resets.

title: Rate-limit counter is per-account and does not bleed between accounts
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
User account 'alice' has exhausted the three-request limit
User account 'bob' has made zero password-reset requests
steps: POST /api/auth/password-reset with body {"username":"bob"}
expected_result: HTTP 200 or HTTP 202 response; bob's request succeeds because alice's counter does not affect bob's limit.

title: State-changing request with valid CSRF token is accepted
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Student 'alice' has a valid session and a valid CSRF token obtained from the server
steps: Send POST /api/student/courses with alice's session token and the valid CSRF token in the X-CSRF-Token header
Include a valid course creation payload in the request body
expected_result: HTTP 200 or HTTP 201 response; the state-changing request is processed successfully.

title: State-changing request with missing CSRF token is rejected
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Student 'alice' has a valid session token
steps: Send POST /api/student/courses with alice's session token but without an X-CSRF-Token header
Include a valid course creation payload in the request body
expected_result: HTTP 403 response; the request is rejected before any state change is applied.

title: State-changing request with invalid CSRF token is rejected
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Student 'alice' has a valid session token
steps: Send POST /api/student/courses with alice's session token and the value 'invalid-csrf-token' in the X-CSRF-Token header
Include a valid course creation payload in the request body
expected_result: HTTP 403 response; the request is rejected before any state change is applied.

title: Read-only GET request is not blocked when CSRF token is absent
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Student 'alice' has a valid session token
steps: Send GET /api/student/courses with alice's session token and no X-CSRF-Token header
expected_result: HTTP 200 response; read-only requests are not subject to CSRF token validation and are processed normally.

title: CSRF token validation unit test rejects mismatched token
steps: Call the CSRF validation function with session-bound expected token 'tok-abc' and presented token 'tok-xyz'
expected_result: The function returns a validation failure; the tokens do not match and the call is not authorized.

title: Replayed CSRF token on a second request in the same session is rejected with HTTP 403
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Student 'alice' has a valid session token
A valid single-use CSRF token has been obtained from the server for alice's session
steps: Send POST /api/student/courses with alice's session token and the valid CSRF token in the X-CSRF-Token header; include a valid course creation payload — assert HTTP 200 or HTTP 201
Send POST /api/student/courses again with alice's session token and the same CSRF token value in the X-CSRF-Token header; include a valid course creation payload
expected_result: HTTP 403 response on the second request; the server rejects the replayed CSRF token because it is single-use or bound to the prior request context, even though the token passes signature and format checks.

title: CSRF token from Student A's session is rejected when presented with Student B's auth token
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Student 'alice' has a valid session token and has obtained a valid CSRF token bound to her session
Student 'bob' has a valid session token
steps: Send POST /api/student/courses with bob's session token in the Authorization header and alice's CSRF token in the X-CSRF-Token header; include a valid course creation payload
expected_result: HTTP 403 response; the server rejects the request because the CSRF token is bound to alice's session and does not match bob's session context.

title: Student B cannot submit to a course owned by Student A via POST submissions endpoint
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Student account 'alice' owns course with id 'course-alice-1'
Student account 'bob' has a valid session token and a valid CSRF token
steps: Send POST /api/student/courses/course-alice-1/submissions with bob's session token in the Authorization header and a valid submission payload
Query the database to check whether any submission record was created for course-alice-1 by bob
expected_result: HTTP 403 response; no submission record is created in the database for course-alice-1 attributed to bob or any other student.

title: Student B cannot post a chat message to a course owned by Student A via POST chat endpoint
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Student account 'alice' owns course with id 'course-alice-1'
Student account 'bob' has a valid session token and a valid CSRF token
steps: Send POST /api/student/courses/course-alice-1/chat with bob's session token in the Authorization header and a valid chat message payload
Query the database to check whether any chat record was created for course-alice-1 by bob
expected_result: HTTP 403 response; no chat record is created in the database for course-alice-1 attributed to bob or any other student.
